jobs.title: Inside Sales Contractor
jobs.jd_full:
About Us

We are a financial services start up focusing on helping to improve consumer credit profiles. We are currently seeking KPI driven sales representatives looking to earn up to 45K in their first year while working remotely. We offer comprehensive training and continuous sales coaching to help you meet your financial goals.

During our training period we offer a guaranteed training stipend while our trainees are acclimating to the position (*see weekly pay below). If you are a seasoned sales professional looking for the autonomy of a remote position combined with great compensation, we want to hear from you!

Compensation Structure

This role is 100% commission-based, which means your earning potential is unlimited. In addition, we regularly offer competitive performance-based bonuses to reward hard work and results.

Training Period (Weeks 1–4)

We invest in your success and want to make sure you’re supported as you get up to speed:

  • Week 1: Commission-only (a chance to start earning right away while learning the ropes).

  • Weeks 2–4: Guaranteed training stipend of $1,000 total – or your commission if it’s higher. You’ll always receive whichever amount benefits you most.

    • Week 2: $250 guaranteed minimum

    • Week 3: $325 guaranteed minimum

    • Week 4: $425 guaranteed minimum

By the end of training, you’ll have the skills to maximize commissions, with the safety net of a guaranteed base during your ramp-up period.

Post Training Period:

  • Average first year OTE: 25K-35K (US) Annually

  • Top Rep first year OTE: 35K-45K (US) Annually

*The above is the average pay you can expect, however, there is unlimited earning potential for those who are financially motivated top performers looking to exceed sales targets.

What will you be doing?

Educating inbound callers on their credit standing by providing consultations with the goal of enrolling them in one of our services should they be a good fit.

We are looking for team members who are:

  • Tech savvy with the ability to navigate digital tools such as SLACK, CRM software, google sheets, etc. Our team uses these digital tools daily.

  • Growth oriented and always looking to learn and acquire new skills.

  • Autonomous self starters who can work independently and efficiently.

  • Team players with the ability to implement feedback from their sales coaches.

  • Patient and professional with clients. Finances can be a difficult topic for some clients to discuss. We are looking for individuals who showcase empathy and professionalism especially under pressure!

  • Financially motivated individuals who can meet and exceed sales targets.

  • Outgoing with outstanding rapport building and active listening skills. Can you carry a conversation with anyone? This may be for you!

You will need:

  • Home office or a quiet place to work.

  • Strong internet connection.

  • Desktop or laptop

(*Please Note: Devices such as Chromebooks, IPADs and laptops with 8G’s of RAM and under will have issues supporting our system)

Scheduling

Training: The schedule for the first four weeks of our training period is 10 AM - 6 PM EST.

*The first week of training is mandatory for all new trainees. If a day is missed you may be asked to restart the first week of training.

Post Training:

We are currently seeking representatives to work on the following schedule:

Monday - Friday

Shift hours to be discussed with our hiring manager based on our company's needs at the time of hire. Shifts are as follows:

  • 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM EST

  • 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM EST

  • 9:00 AM - 5:00 AM EST

  • 9:30 AM - 5:30 PM EST

  • 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM EST

  • 10:30 AM - 6:30 PM EST

  • 11:00 AM - 7:00 PM EST

  • 11:30 AM - 7:30 PM EST

  • 12:30 PM - 8:30 PM EST

  • 1:30 PM - 9:30 PM EST

  • 2:30 PM - 10:30 PM EST

  • 3:00 PM - 11:00 PM EST

Job Type: Contract

Shift:

  • 8 hour shift

  • Day shift

  • Evening shift

  • Morning shift

  • Night shift

Supplemental Pay:

  • Bonus opportunities

  • Commission pay

Experience:

  • Sales: 1 year (Required)

Work Location: Remote

jobs.title: Technical Program Manager - Adobe 3D & Immersive (Remote work possible)
jobs.jd_full:
The opportunity

We are looking for a Technical Program Manager to lead the delivery of customized 3D digital twin automation solutions, for some of Adobe’s largest enterprise customers!

This includes marketing & communications agencies, CPG, Electronics or Automotive companies. Joining the Enterprise Solutions & Delivery team (within the Substance 3D Growth organization), this person will own the end-to-end execution of complex technical engagements – ensuring customers successfully deploy and realize value from our solutions. They will also serve as the primary point of contact for delivery, bridging the gap between customers and our product and engineering team.

What you’ll do

  • Own the planning, scope, and execution of digital twin implementation engagements across multiple simultaneous customer accounts

  • Define project pace, manage timelines, and track deliverables against project plan or contract – including measuring the return on investment value of what has been delivered.

  • Be the orchestrator between the customer, product, engineering and technical artist team. This includes coordinating what is being built by engineers and/or TA’s.

  • Coordinate and lead customer-facing project meetings, steering committees, and executive reviews. Their credibility in doing this relies mainly on their 3D pipeline knowledge, technical skill, and problem-solving mentality.

  • Work closely with solutions architect to translate customer requirements into executable delivery plans, setting the right expectations early-on.

  • Build positive relationships with customer partners and internal teams – including being the voice of the customer to our product team.

  • Identify risks early and drive resolution before they impact delivery

  • Participate in the hand-off from pre-sales to post-sales for a smooth transition, maintaining a positive relationship with the Adobe sales team.

What you need to succeed

  • 5+ years of work experience involving technical project management in a software development context or technical professional services, involving API’s.

  • A software engineering background – you have written production code and can hold your own in technical conversations with engineers.

  • Demonstrated delivery of complex, multi-stakeholder technical projects successfully – able to turn ambiguous customer needs into bounded, executable work.

  • Strong knowledge of the 3D production pipeline in an enterprise context and architecture, including hands-on experience with 3D data.

  • A pro-active, customer focused, problem solving perspective

  • Strong project management fundamentals

  • Comfortable in your ability to present to customers, develop relationships and manage expectations.

  • Team-player state of mind – we are a globally distributed, multicultural bunch who want to succeed together!

About Adobe

Adobe empowers everyone to create through innovative platforms and tools that unleash creativity, productivity and personalized customer experiences. Adobe’s industry-leading offerings including Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Firefly, Creative Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, and GenStudio enable people and businesses to turn ideas into impact, powered by AI and driven by human ingenuity.

Our 30,000+ employees worldwide are creating the future and raising the bar as we drive the next decade of growth. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. At Adobe, we believe that great ideas can come from anywhere in the organization. The next big idea could be yours.


Let’s Adobe together

At Adobe, we believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. Learn more about Adobe life, including our values and culture, focus on people, purpose and community, Adobe for All, comprehensive benefits programs, the stories we tell, the customers we serve, and how you can help us advance our mission of empowering everyone to create.

Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Learn more.

Adobe aims to make our Careers website and recruiting process accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call +1 408-536-3015.

AI Use Guidelines for Interviews:
Our interviews are designed to reflect your own skills and thinking. The use of AI or recording tools during live interviews is not permitted unless explicitly invited by the interviewer or approved in advance as part of a reasonable accommodation. If these tools are used inappropriately or in a way that misrepresents your work, your application may not move forward in the process.

At Adobe, we empower employees to innovate with AI — and we look for candidates eager to do the same. As part of the hiring experience, we provide clear guidance on where AI is encouraged during the process and where it’s restricted during live interviews. See how we think about AI in the hiring experience.

Expected Pay Range:

Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several  U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. The U.S. pay range for this position is $124,400 -- $226,900 annually. Pay within this range varies by work location and may also depend on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process.In California, the pay range for this position is $156,700 - $226,900In New York, the pay range for this position is $156,700 - $226,900

At Adobe, for sales roles starting salaries are expressed as total target compensation (TTC = base + commission), and short-term incentives are in the form of sales commission plans. Non-sales roles starting salaries are expressed as base salary and short-term incentives are in the form of the Annual Incentive Plan (AIP).

In addition, certain roles may be eligible for long-term incentives in the form of a new hire equity award.

State-Specific Notices:

California:

Fair Chance Ordinances

Adobe will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records for employment in accordance with state and local laws and “fair chance” ordinances.

Colorado:

Application Window Notice

If this role is open to hiring in Colorado (as listed on the job posting), the application window will remain open until at least the date and time stated above in Pacific Time, in compliance with Colorado pay transparency regulations. If this role does not have Colorado listed as a hiring location, no specific application window applies, and the posting may close at any time based on hiring needs.

Massachusetts:

Massachusetts Legal Notice

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: ServiceNow Software Asset Management (SAM) Product/Program Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Type of Requisition:

Regular

Clearance Level Must Currently Possess:

None

Clearance Level Must Be Able to Obtain:

None

Public Trust/Other Required:

NACI (T1)

Job Family:

Software Engineering

Job Qualifications:

Skills:

Collaboration, Deliverables Management, Information Technology Project Management, SAM Software

Certifications:

None

Experience:

5 + years of related experience

US Citizenship Required:

No

Job Description:

Seize your opportunity to make a personal impact as a ServiceNow SAM Product/Program Manager supporting Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services - Cloud Products & Tools (CMS - CPT). GDIT is your place to make meaningful contributions to challenging projects and grow a rewarding career.

At GDIT, people are our differentiator. As a ServiceNow SAM Product/Program Manager is responsible for the strategy, roadmap, and delivery of Software Asset Management capabilities on the ServiceNow platform. This role drives business value by partnering closely with stakeholders to effectively manage the successful planning and completion of contract objectives, tasks, and the supervision of program team members, perform PMO activities. Additionally, as Product Manager, you will work closely with IT Asset Management (ITAM), engineering teams, and business stakeholders to deliver scalable, compliant, and data-driven SAM solutions using ServiceNow best practices, including configuration over customization and alignment with the Common Service Data Model (CSDM).

In alignment with CMS' strategic initiative to streamline asset management, achieve compliance, and optimize IT costs, this role supports the agency’s mission by leveraging advanced IT Asset Management (ITAM) processes to ensure accurate asset tracking, operational transparency, and regulatory adherence. This individual mentors team members, collaborates across CMS departments and vendors, and establishes an effective process for value delivery and timely ROI.

HOW YOU WILL MAKE AN IMPACT:

Program Management:

  • Directs program activities to meet client and organization work objectives and serves as a liaison with clients to coordinate activities, negotiate tasks, and solve problems

  • Track financial forecasts, lifecycle costs, and budgetary alignment across SAM initiatives
  • Performs financial management services, including financial analysis, budget and cost quote preparation, cost management, reconciliation, and profit enhancement
  • Establish and maintain a documented set of disciplined, mature, and continuously improving processes for administering all contractual efforts with an emphasis on cost-efficiency, schedule, performance, responsiveness, resource management, and consistently high-quality delivery
  • Coordinate across CMS stakeholders, procurement teams, technical groups (Infrastructure, Security, Hosting, DBA)
  • Develop, deliver and maintain and ensure compliance with the Project Management Plan and backlogs
  • Provide the government with monthly and quarterly status reports on the activities and milestones completed and deliverables submitted for each task for the past month
  • Ensure alignment of program deliverables to program outcomes for operational efficiency
  • Coordinates and monitors sub-contractor activities and administers subcontractor deliverables and finances
  • Coach SAM team members to foster skill development and alignment with CMS standards

Product Management:

  • Define and communicate the product vision for ServiceNow SAM, aligned with enterprise IT and financial objectives
  • Develop and maintain a roadmap covering key capabilities such as license compliance, SaaS management, and software optimization.
  • Own and prioritize the SAM product backlog based on business value, risk, and stakeholder needs.
  • Translate business requirements into clear user stories with acceptance criteria.
  • Balance new feature delivery with technical debt reduction and platform sustainability.
  • Align SAM implementation with the Common Service Data Model (CSDM) and CMDB standards.
  • Partner with ITAM, Procurement, Finance, Security, and IT Operations to gather requirements and define priorities.
  • Act as the primary point of contact for SAM product-related decisions.
  • Communicate roadmap updates, releases, and value delivered to stakeholders.
  • Ensure a Continual Service Improvement mechanism in-place for SAM
  • Participate in sprint planning, backlog refinement, and product reviews
  • Collaborate closely with developers, architects, and QA teams to ensure high-quality delivery.
  • Collaborate with Enterprise IT Asset Management, Hosting, Security, DBA, Operations, and Technical teams to implement asset management practices, processes, and procedures
  • Deliver SAM application status overviews, asset compliance updates, and optimization recommendations to stakeholders
  • Interface with vendors for license renewals, contracts, and hardware/software lifecycle updates
  • Ensure adherence to governance and compliance standards throughout project phases
  • Oversee team activities and track progress against project timelines and deliverables
  • Define and monitor SAM KPIs
  • Deliver dashboards and reporting for operational and executive insights.
  • Drive adoption of advanced SAM features (e.g., SaaS License Management, publisher packs).
  • Continuously evaluate platform usage and recommend enhancements.
  • Support vendor audit readiness (e.g., Microsoft, Oracle, Adobe).
  • Identify and mitigate compliance risks through proactive monitoring.
  • Ensure adherence to internal governance and regulatory requirements.
  • Track Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and maintain dashboards to measure team performance and operational success

WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO SUCCEED:

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in information systems, Business, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical discipline, or the equivalent combination of education, technical certifications or training, or work experience
  • Required Experience: 5 + years of IT project management experience, with 2 years in SAM or ITAM projects
  • Required Technical Skills:
  • Experience as a Technical Program Manager with direct oversight of labor, materials, financials, and IT system/platform
  • Understanding of SAM/ITAM principles and their integration into enterprise infrastructure
  • Demonstrated success in implementing SAM/ITAM initiatives across enterprise environments
  • Certified Project Management Professional or equivalent
  • Experience in project management tools (e.g., MS Project, PPM, Agile 2.0, Jira, Confluence)
  • Experience with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps concepts
  • Advanced stakeholder management and communication skills, including conflict resolution
  • Expertise in risk management and development of mitigation strategies
  • Proactive leadership in process improvement and automation
  • Attributes for Success: Strong leadership; Team coordination; Process improvement skills
  • Preferred Skills: 8 + years of IT project management experience; Certified Software Asset Management (CSAM) certification or equivalent; Certified Hardware Asset Management Professional (CHAMP) certification or equivalent; Experience with automation and integration across IT management platforms, including ServiceNow Pro Plus AI Agents; Experience with COTS Product lifecycle management, procurement workflows, contract management, and vendor coordination; Experience working on complex, large-scale IT programs or enterprise-grade data centers
  • Location: Remote 100%
  • Clearance: Requires ability to pass CMS background check and meet the residency requirement for having resided in the US at least (3) three out of the last (5) five years
  • Work visa sponsorship will not be considered for this role

GDIT IS YOUR PLACE:
At GDIT, the mission is our purpose, and our people are at the center of everything we do.

  • Growth: AI-powered career tool that identifies career steps and learning opportunities
  • Support: An internal mobility team focused on helping you achieve your career goals
  • Rewards: Comprehensive benefits and wellness packages, 401K with company match, and competitive pay and paid time off
  • Flexibility: Full-flex work week to own your priorities at work and at home
  • Community: Award-winning culture of innovation and a military-friendly workplace

#GDITFedHealthJobs

#GDITHealthSystems

The likely salary range for this position is $148,750 - $201,250. This is not, however, a guarantee of compensation or salary. Rather, salary will be set based on experience, geographic location and possibly contractual requirements and could fall outside of this range.

Scheduled Weekly Hours:

40

Travel Required:

Less than 10%

Telecommuting Options:

Remote

Work Location:

Any Location / Remote

Additional Work Locations:

Total Rewards at GDIT:

Our benefits package for all US-based employees includes a variety of medical plan options, some with Health Savings Accounts, dental plan options, a vision plan, and a 401(k) plan offering the ability to contribute both pre and post-tax dollars up to the IRS annual limits and receive a company match. To encourage work/life balance, GDIT offers employees full flex work weeks where possible and a variety of paid time off plans, including vacation, sick and personal time, holidays, paid parental, military, bereavement and jury duty leave. GDIT typically provides new employees with 15 days of paid leave per calendar year to be used for vacations, personal business, and illness and an additional 10 paid holidays per year. Paid leave and paid holidays are prorated based on the employee’s date of hire. The GDIT Paid Family Leave program provides a total of up to 160 hours of paid leave in a rolling 12 month period for eligible employees. To ensure our employees are able to protect their income, other offerings such as short and long-term disability benefits, life, accidental death and dismemberment, personal accident, critical illness and business travel and accident insurance are provided or available. We regularly review our Total Rewards package to ensure our offerings are competitive and reflect what our employees have told us they value most.We are GDIT. A global technology and professional services company that delivers consulting, technology and mission services to every major agency across the U.S. government, defense and intelligence community. Our 30,000 experts extract the power of technology to create immediate value and deliver solutions at the edge of innovation. We operate across 50 countries worldwide, offering leading capabilities in digital modernization, AI/ML, Cloud, Cyber and application development. Together with our clients, we strive to create a safer, smarter world by harnessing the power of deep expertise and advanced technology.Join our Talent Community to stay up to date on our career opportunities and events at

gdit.com/tc.

Equal Opportunity Employer / Individuals with Disabilities / Protected Veterans
